title: Note for the neighbour
prompt: Write a short note in Norwegian to a neighbour.

payload.stimulus: You will be away this weekend. Write a short note (en lapp) in Norwegian to your neighbour Kari. Say that you travel on Friday, ask her to water your plants (vanne blomstene), and say you are back Sunday evening.
criteria: Uses a friendly greeting and closing (Hei Kari / Tusen takk)
States the days clearly (fredag, søndag kveld)
Makes one simple polite request using 'Kan du...?'
Uses correct present-tense verbs at A1-A2 level

title: Library sign-up form
prompt: Fill in a simple library form in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: You are signing up at the local library (biblioteket). Write the form answers in Norwegian: navn, adresse, telefonnummer, fødselsår, and one sentence about which books you like (Jeg liker...).
criteria: Provides all requested personal details in Norwegian labels
Writes numbers and address in a readable Norwegian format
Ends with one full sentence about book preferences
Uses 'jeg liker' correctly

title: Message: running late
prompt: Write a short text message to a friend in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: You planned to meet your friend Mikkel at the café at 15:00, but the bus is delayed. Send a short message (en melding) in Norwegian: apologise, say you will be about 20 minutes late, and suggest he orders a coffee.
criteria: Apologises naturally (Beklager / Unnskyld)
Gives a clear new time or delay (cirka 20 minutter)
Makes a simple suggestion (Du kan gjerne bestille...)
Keeps a warm, informal tone with a friend

title: Shopping list with a note
prompt: Write a shopping list and a short note in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: Your flatmate is going to the shop (butikken). Write a short Norwegian note: list four things you need (for example melk, brød, egg, smør) and ask them to buy fruit if it is cheap (hvis det er billig).
criteria: Lists at least four everyday food items in Norwegian
Includes a short introductory line to the flatmate
Uses a simple conditional idea (hvis det er billig)
Spelling of common nouns is close to correct

title: Postcard from a day trip
prompt: Write a short postcard in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: You spent a day in Bergen. Write a short postcard (et postkort) in Norwegian to your cousin: say where you are, what the weather is like, and one thing you did (for example, I saw the fish market / Jeg så fisketorget).
criteria: Opens with a postcard greeting (Kjære...)
Mentions the place and the weather (været)
Describes one past activity simply
Ends with a friendly sign-off (Klem / Hilsen)

title: Email to the language school
prompt: Write a short email in Norwegian to your school.

payload.stimulus: You cannot attend your Norwegian class on Thursday because you have a doctor's appointment (en time hos legen). Write a short, polite email in Norwegian to your teacher: explain why, and ask what homework (lekser) you should do.
criteria: Uses a suitable email greeting (Hei / Kjære lærer)
Gives a clear, simple reason for the absence
Asks one polite question about homework
Closes politely (Vennlig hilsen + name)

title: Describe your kitchen
prompt: Write a few sentences describing your kitchen in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: Describe your kitchen (kjøkkenet ditt) in Norwegian. Write three to four short sentences: name a few things in it (kjøleskap, bord, stoler), say what you often cook, and say when you usually eat dinner.
criteria: Names at least three items found in a kitchen
Uses 'det er' to say what is in the room
Includes a habit with a time (Jeg spiser klokka...)
Sentences stay short and grammatically simple

payload.stimulus: It is your first day in a Norwegian class. Introduce yourself: say your name, where you come from, where you live now, and one hobby (en fritidsinteresse).
criteria: Says name and country clearly (Jeg heter... Jeg kommer fra...)
States current town or city of residence
Mentions one hobby with a simple verb
Speaks in short, complete Norwegian sentences

title: Order at a bakery
prompt: Order food at a Norwegian bakery.

payload.stimulus: You are at a bakery (et bakeri). Order two things (for example a loaf of bread and two buns), ask the price, and say thank you when you pay.
criteria: Uses a polite ordering phrase (Jeg vil gjerne ha...)
Names two bakery items in Norwegian
Asks the price (Hva koster det?)
Closes the exchange politely (Takk / Ha det)

payload.stimulus: Describe your typical morning in Norwegian. Say when you get up, what you eat and drink for breakfast, and how you travel to work or school.
criteria: Uses a time to say when you wake up (Jeg står opp klokka...)
Names breakfast food and drink
Describes the way to work or school (buss, sykkel, går)
Links steps with words like 'og' and 'så'

title: Talk about your family
prompt: Talk about your family in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: Talk about your family (familien din). Say who is in it, one thing about each person (age, job, or where they live), and who you see most often.
criteria: Names at least two family members with the right words
Gives one detail per person (alder, arbeid, eller by)
Uses 'har' correctly (Jeg har en søster...)
Speaks for the full time without long silences

payload.stimulus: You are lost in town and want to find the train station (togstasjonen). Stop a friendly person: greet them, ask the way politely, and thank them.
criteria: Opens politely (Unnskyld, kan du hjelpe meg?)
Asks clearly for the destination (Hvor er togstasjonen?)
Uses one direction word (til høyre, til venstre, rett fram)
Thanks the person at the end

title: Plan the weekend with a friend

payload.stimulus: A friend asks what you want to do this weekend. Suggest one activity (for example a walk in the park or a film), say which day and time, and ask if it suits them.
criteria: Proposes an activity (Skal vi gå en tur / se en film?)
Names a day and a time
Asks whether it fits (Passer det for deg?)
Responds naturally as in a short conversation

title: At the doctor's reception
prompt: Explain a simple problem at the doctor's.

payload.stimulus: You are at the doctor's reception (hos legen). Greet the receptionist, say you have a cold and a headache (forkjølelse og hodepine), and ask for an appointment today.
criteria: Greets and gives your name
Describes two simple symptoms in Norwegian
Asks for an appointment (Kan jeg få en time i dag?)
Uses 'jeg har' correctly with body words

title: Describe your first day at work
prompt: Talk about starting a new job in Norwegian.

payload.stimulus: Talk about your first day at a new job (den nye jobben din). Say where you work, what time you start, one task you do, and one colleague you met.
criteria: Says the workplace and start time
Describes one everyday work task simply
Mentions a colleague (en kollega) and something about them
Uses present tense verbs consistently
